channel.title: Home on seigler.github.io
channel.description: Recent content in Home on seigler.github.io

item.title: About
item.description: Hi, I’m Joshua Seigler. I’m a: Web developer I like working with web technology. Since 2017 I work full time for Dash Core Inc, which develops the Dash cryptocurrency. Front-end web technology like CSS, HTML, and SVG comes most easily to me, but I’m also learning to solve problems with Javascript, especially React. See some of my personal projects. Cryptocurrency enthusiast Dash and many other blockchain applications are just starting to change the world.

item.title: Site redesign!
item.description: I rebuilt the site with a from-scratch Hugo theme. Styles are assembled from LESS with a Gulp task runner. Afterwards, static assets are revision-hashed, and markup is reformatted. Hugo Hugo is a super fast, easily installed static site generator. It’s a lot like Jekyll, GitHub’s Ruby-powered static site generator. But it brings with it a lot of the Golang philosophy, and unlike Jekyll, Hugo doesn’t let you twist its arm into doing a lot of extra stuff like image processing or stylesheet building.

item.title: VPS the Hard Way
item.description: Title slide What is a VPS? It stands for Virtual Private Server. Basically you get access to a virtual machine running with dozens of others in a rack in a server room somewhere with fast internet. Since it’s a virtual machine you can run whatever you want. It’s cheap, it’s unsupported, and it’s all yours. Riddle me this! Why? What good is it? Think back to when you first discovered the internet.
